findings.journal: The Journal of neuroscience : the official journal of the Society for Neuroscience
findings.evidence: Conductance-based models fit to in vitro ephys; intrinsic differences predict layer-specific disinhibitory effects.

findings.title: VIP interneurons in mouse primary visual cortex selectively enhance responses to weak but specific stimuli.
findings.claim: Combined V1 imaging + circuit model demonstrates that VIP interneuron activation produces substantial gain enhancement of weak (low-contrast) sensory responses through disinhibition of pyramidal cells, despite low net activity of intermediate SST cells.
findings.claim_source_sentence: With respect to the conclusion – "These results indicate that Vip-mediated disinhibition is capable of producing substantial increases in gain at low contrast despite low activity of the intermediate SST neuron population." The authors should explain why this is not a circular argument and a strange way to summarize the results of the model.
findings.authors: Millman DJ, Ocker GK, Caldejon S, Kato I, Larkin JD, Lee EK, Luviano J, Nayan C, Nguyen TV, North K, Seid S, White C, Lecoq J, Reid C, Buice MA, de Vries SE.
findings.evidence: Optogenetic VIP activation + rate model with PV, SST, VIP populations; gain modulation curves match experimental contrast tuning.

findings.title: Cell type-specific inhibitory inputs to dendritic and somatic compartments of parvalbumin-expressing neocortical interneuron.
findings.claim: Quantitative anatomical analysis of inhibitory innervation of pyramidal cells reveals cell-type-specific synaptic compartmentalization (PV→soma, SST→dendrite, VIP→other interneurons), providing the structural foundation that all subsequent three-interneuron computational models rely on.
findings.claim_source_sentence: We then investigated inhibitory inputs from three distinct subgroups of GABAergic interneurons by visualizing the axon terminals immunopositive for PV, somatostatin (SOM), or vasoactive intestinal polypeptide (VIP).
findings.authors: Hioki H, Okamoto S, Konno M, Kameda H, Sohn J, Kuramoto E, Fujiyama F, Kaneko T.
findings.journal: The Journal of neuroscience : the official journal of the Society for Neuroscience
findings.evidence: Immunohistochemical mapping of PV/SST/VIP synapses on PV-EGFP cell types; quantitative connectivity matrix.

findings.title: Disinhibitory Circuitry Gates Associative Synaptic Plasticity in Olfactory Cortex.
findings.claim: Olfactory cortex circuit model + slice physiology shows that LTP at associational synapses is gated by VIP-mediated disinhibition, with VIP activation and SST/PV inactivation jointly required for plasticity induction.
findings.claim_source_sentence: We find that LTP is gated by the inactivation SST or PV interneurons and by the activation of VIP interneurons.
findings.authors: Canto-Bustos M, Friason FK, Bassi C, Oswald AM.
findings.journal: The Journal of neuroscience : the official journal of the Society for Neuroscience
findings.evidence: Brain-slice optogenetics + microcircuit simulation; LTP threshold quantitatively controlled by VIP recruitment.

findings.title: Visual processing mode switching regulated by VIP cells.
findings.claim: Three-interneuron PFC column model (PV, SST, VIP + pyramidal) reproduces visual-processing-mode switching, with VIP-driven disinhibition implementing top-down state changes between feedforward and feedback regimes.
findings.claim_source_sentence: The computational model used here is an extension of our earlier model 13 , in which, PV, SST and VIP cells in the superficial layers of 13 columns interact with one another and with Pyr cells via cell-type specific connections within and across columns.
findings.authors: Lee JH, Mihalas S.
findings.evidence: Multi-column rate model with cell-class-specific synapses; tested two visual processing states under VIP modulation.

findings.title: Excitatory/inhibitory ratio disruption modulates neural synchrony and flow directions in a cortical microcircuit.
findings.claim: Spiking E/I network with VIP→SOM disinhibition shows that disrupting E/I ratio modulates synchrony and information flow directions, supporting top-down attentional modulation via the VIP-SOM gating mechanism.
findings.claim_source_sentence: Recent computational studies suggest that local disinhibition by connections from VIP to SOM interneuron populations may be a plausible mechanism for the top-down attentional modulation of responses in the cortical microcircuit in layers 2/3 of the V1 [ 31 , 33 ].
findings.authors: Wagatsuma N, Nobukawa S, Kurikawa T.
findings.evidence: Large-scale spiking simulation with parameterized E/I imbalance; transfer-entropy analyses confirm flow modulation.

findings.title: Learning and attention increase visual response selectivity through distinct mechanisms.
findings.claim: Combined imaging + circuit model demonstrates that learning and attention enhance V1 selectivity via dissociable mechanisms — attention through VIP-mediated disinhibitory gain, learning through plasticity at PV/SST synapses.
findings.claim_source_sentence: As reported earlier, we found that during learning, SOM cells become de-correlated from pyramidal, PV, and VIP neurons, with the largest changes between cell classes (sign test, all reductions in noise correlation were significant at p < 10 −4 [Bonferroni corrected all p < 10 −3 ], with the exception of SOM-SOM cell pairs, p = 0.75, sign test [see also Khan et al., 2018 ]).
findings.authors: Poort J, Wilmes KA, Blot A, Chadwick A, Sahani M, Clopath C, Mrsic-Flogel TD, Hofer SB, Khan AG.
findings.evidence: Two-photon imaging in V1 with optogenetic perturbations + rate model; double dissociation of effects on Pyr selectivity.

findings.title: Propofol-mediated loss of consciousness disrupts predictive routing and local field phase modulation of neural activity.
findings.claim: Mean-field whole-cortex model with PV/SST/VIP interneurons explains propofol-induced loss of consciousness as failure of feedback predictive routing through VIP-disinhibitory gating.
findings.claim_source_sentence: This led to a disinhibition of gamma/spiking, consistent with the predictive routing model.
findings.authors: Xiong YS, Donoghue JA, Lundqvist M, Mahnke M, Major AJ, Brown EN, Miller EK, Bastos AM.
findings.journal: Proceedings of the National Academy of Sciences of the United States of America
findings.evidence: Coupled cortical regions modeled with cell-class-specific GABA-A; anesthesia simulated via potentiation of GABA shifts predictive flow.

findings.title: Paradoxical response reversal of top-down modulation in cortical circuits with three interneuron types.
findings.claim: Three-interneuron model (PV, SST, VIP) reveals paradoxical effect of top-down inputs: VIP recruitment can either disinhibit or super-inhibit pyramidals depending on operating regime determined by SST drive.
findings.claim_source_sentence: This observation provides an explanation to the reversal of the response of SST to VIP activation when the baseline activity is changed: as we show in Figure 2a and c for low baseline activity, M S ⁢ V is negative and the presence of an external excitatory current targeting VIP cells will result in a negative response of SST cells and positive response of E, PV and VIP cells, conforming to the disinhibitory hypothesis.
findings.authors: Garcia Del Molino LC, Yang GR, Mejias JF, Wang XJ.
findings.evidence: Linearized rate analysis + nonlinear simulation of canonical 3IN circuit; bifurcation diagram across SST drive.

findings.title: Cell-type-specific plasticity of inhibitory interneurons in the rehabilitation of auditory cortex after peripheral damage.
findings.claim: Circuit model with PV/SST/VIP populations shows that interneuron-class-specific plasticity supports recovery from sensory deprivation; VIP-driven dis-inhibition opens critical periods for circuit refinement.
findings.claim_source_sentence: These findings, along with our computational modelling results, are consistent with the notion that PV plasticity contributes to PN stability, SOM plasticity allows for increased PN and PV activity, and VIP plasticity enables PN and PV recovery by inhibiting SOMs.
findings.authors: Kumar M, Handy G, Kouvaros S, Zhao Y, Brinson LL, Wei E, Bizup B, Doiron B, Tzounopoulos T.
findings.evidence: Spiking microcircuit with STDP-like learning rules; ablation of VIP→SST plasticity prevents critical-period recovery.

findings.title: Role of interneuron subtypes in controlling trial-by-trial output variability in the neocortex.
findings.claim: Modeling of cortical interneuron subtypes (PV, SST, VIP, NDNF) shows class-specific contributions to trial-by-trial variability, with VIP recruitment increasing variability through gain modulation.
findings.claim_source_sentence: To address this, we investigate the dynamics of stimulus response in a cortical microcircuit model with one excitatory and three inhibitory interneuron populations (PV, SST, VIP).
findings.authors: Guo L, Kumar A.
findings.evidence: Spiking 4-population model fitted to V1 imaging variance statistics across conditions.

findings.title: Cortical Interneurons Differentially Shape Frequency Tuning following Adaptation.
findings.claim: Auditory cortex circuit model with PV, SOM and VIP interneurons explains adaptation and frequency-tuning specificity through cell-type-specific divisive vs subtractive inhibition combined with VIP-driven gain control.
findings.claim_source_sentence: After adaptation, suppressing SOM activity caused predominantly disinhibitory effects, whereas suppressing PV activity still evoked bi-directional changes.
findings.authors: Natan RG, Rao W, Geffen MN.
findings.evidence: Rate model fit to auditory cortex tuning curves; VIP recruitment shifts tuning sharpness.

findings.title: A circuit model of auditory cortex.
findings.claim: Auditory-cortex spiking model with three interneuron classes shows that VIP-mediated disinhibition shapes context-dependent responses and explains experience-dependent plasticity.
findings.claim_source_sentence: The three-unit model was developed to reproduce the compensating mechanisms of the single-unit model: PV suppression results in constant disinhibition for repeated tones, and SST suppression results in a compensating effect from PVs before adaptation that weakens as adaptation strengthens.
findings.authors: Park Y, Geffen MN.
findings.evidence: Conductance-based spiking circuit; perturbation of VIP→SST synapse alters predicted tuning shifts.

findings.title: Common rules underlying optogenetic and behavioral modulation of responses in multi-cell-type V1 circuits
findings.claim: Multi-cell-type optogenetic perturbation experiments combined with circuit modeling resolve PV vs SST vs VIP roles in behavior, with VIP-mediated disinhibition specifically gating context-dependent task switches.
findings.claim_source_sentence: We infer inputs that explain locomotion-induced changes in firing rates and find that, contrary to hypotheses of simple disinhibition, locomotory drive to VIP cells and to SOM cells largely cancel, with enhancement of excitatory-cell visual responses likely due to direct locomotory drive to them.
findings.authors: Palmigiano A, Fumarola F, Mossing DP, Kraynyukova N, Adesnik H, Miller KD.
findings.evidence: Optogenetic activation/silencing of PV/SST/VIP + rate model; cell-class-specific effects fitted to behavior.

findings.title: A Role for Somatostatin-Positive Interneurons in Neuro-Oscillatory and Information Processing Deficits in Schizophrenia.
findings.claim: Computational psychiatry model with PV, SST, VIP populations shows that altered SST function disrupts oscillatory information processing relevant to schizophrenia, with VIP modulation partially compensating.
findings.claim_source_sentence: We highlight a growing body of work in rodents, which now indicates that SSTs may also underlie specific aspects of cortical circuit function, namely low-frequency oscillations, disinhibition, and mediation of cortico-cortical feedback.
findings.authors: Van Derveer AB, Bastos G, Ferrell AD, Gallimore CG, Greene ML, Holmes JT, Kubricka V, Ross JM, Hamm JP.
findings.evidence: Spiking model of cortical microcircuit; parameter perturbation of SST simulates pathological state.
findings.study_system: spiking cortical model with SST perturbation

findings.title: Neural Substrates and Models of Omission Responses and Predictive Processes.
findings.claim: Predictive-coding microcircuit model with VIP-disinhibitory motif explains sensory omission responses through top-down prediction errors gated by VIP→SST disinhibition of pyramidal apical dendrites.
findings.claim_source_sentence: The deviant-selective population displays enhanced responses, above the baseline level, due to SST interneurons silencing PV interneurons and consequently disinhibiting the non-adapted excitatory populations that respond to other stimuli than the standard.
findings.authors: Braga A, Schönwiesner M.
findings.evidence: Two-compartment pyramidal model + 3IN circuit simulating omission paradigms; VIP-recruitment dynamics control omission response amplitude.

findings.title: Inhibitory microcircuits for top-down plasticity of sensory representations.
findings.claim: Top-down inhibitory plasticity model with PV/SST/VIP interneurons demonstrates that feedback projections to VIP cells implement supervisor signals for cortical microcircuit learning.
findings.claim_source_sentence: In our model, stimulus-tuned somatostatin-positive interneurons develop strong connections to parvalbumin-positive interneurons during reward such that they selectively disinhibit the pyramidal layer henceforth.
findings.authors: Wilmes KA, Clopath C.
findings.evidence: Plasticity rules across multiple interneuron types in spiking model; VIP-gated supervised learning emerges.

findings.title: Breaking Balance: Encoding local error signals in perturbations of excitation-inhibition balance
findings.claim: Predictive-coding spiking circuit with E-PV-SST-VIP architecture encodes prediction errors as perturbations of E-I balance, with VIP-disinhibition implementing top-down learning signals that break tight balance locally.
findings.claim_source_sentence: Furthermore, we demonstrate that this framework is consistent with key features of disinhibitory microcircuit dynamics during in-vivo learning experiments.
findings.authors: Rossbroich J, Zenke F.
findings.evidence: 4-population spiking circuit trained with predictive-coding objectives; analysis of E-I balance perturbations as the error code.

findings.title: Flexible control of representational dynamics in a disinhibition-based model of decision-making.
findings.claim: Spiking decision-making model with PV, SST, VIP interneurons shows that VIP-mediated disinhibition flexibly biases winner-take-all attractor dynamics during context-dependent choice.
findings.claim_source_sentence: Vasoactive intestinal peptide (VIP)-expressing interneurons typically inhibit somatostatin (SST) and parvalbumin (PV)-positive interneurons, resulting in a disinhibition of pyramidal neurons.
findings.authors: Shen B, Louie K, Glimcher P.
findings.evidence: Recurrent attractor network with three interneuron types; VIP recruitment shifts choice probabilities.

findings.title: Modeling and simulation of neocortical micro- and mesocircuitry (Part II, Physiology and experimentation).
findings.claim: Detailed multi-cell-type micro/mesocircuit model of mouse primary somatosensory cortex (Part II) with PV, SST and VIP interneurons reproduces in vivo dynamics and reveals VIP-disinhibitory contribution to bottom-up gain.
findings.claim_source_sentence: While the animal model is different, using this data enables comparison to awake responses of layer-wise E populations and I subpopulations (VIP+ [vasoactive intestinal peptide, a part of our 5HT3aR+ subpopulation], Sst+, FS [fast spiking, corresponding to our PV+ subpopulation]).
findings.authors: Isbister JB, Ecker A, Pokorny C, Bolaños-Puchet S, Egas Santander D, Arnaudon A, Awile O, Natali BZ, Alonso JB, Boci E, Chindemi G, Courcol JD, Damart T, Delemontex T, Dietz A, Ficarelli G, Gevaert M, Herttuainen J, Ivaska G, Ji W, Keller D, King J, Kumbhar P, Lapere S, Litvak P, Mandge D, Muller EB, Pereira F, Planas J, Ranjan R, Reva M, Romani A, Rössert C, Schürmann F, Sood V, Teska A, Tuncel A, Van Geit W, Wolf M, Markram H, Ramaswamy S, Reimann MW.
findings.evidence: Biophysically detailed Hodgkin-Huxley NetPyNE model with cell-type connectivity from connectomics; benchmarked against in vivo data.

findings.title: Stability and learning in excitatory synapses by nonlinear inhibitory plasticity.
findings.claim: Theoretical analysis of E-synapse plasticity rules under nonlinear inhibitory feedback (PV/SST/VIP) shows that VIP-mediated disinhibition controls the timing of plasticity windows in cortical microcircuits.
findings.claim_source_sentence: The inhibitory populations in our model can be linked to the two main inhibitory neuron types in the cortex, somatostatin-expressing (SOM) and parvalbumin-expressing (PV) inhibitory interneurons.
findings.authors: Miehl C, Gjorgjieva J.
findings.evidence: Mean-field analysis + simulation of triplet-STDP under multi-class inhibition; VIP gates plasticity efficacy.
findings.study_system: mean-field plasticity model with multiple interneurons

findings.title: Dendritic error backpropagation in deep cortical microcircuits
findings.claim: Dendritic-error backpropagation model in cortex with multi-compartment pyramidals and microcircuit interneurons (PV, SST, VIP) shows that VIP-mediated disinhibition implements approximate gradient-based learning in deep cortical hierarchies.
findings.claim_source_sentence: Finally, complementing this cortical circuit with a disinhibitory mechanism enables attention-like stimulus denoising and generation.
findings.evidence: Theoretical derivation + simulation of multi-layer cortex with cell-type-specific synapses; learning matches backprop in benchmark tasks.

findings.title: The interplay between somatic and dendritic inhibition promotes the emergence and stabilization of place fields.
findings.claim: Spiking microcircuit model demonstrates that emergence of differential somatic vs dendritic inhibition by PV vs SST cells, combined with VIP-mediated disinhibition, produces hierarchical predictive coding signals.
findings.claim_source_sentence: Dendritic disinhibition and synaptic plasticity promote the development of place cells Using our model, we next investigate whether there is an alternative mechanism underlying place field formation from originally silent cells ( Fig 2A and 2B ).
findings.authors: Pedrosa V, Clopath C.
findings.evidence: Spike-timing-dependent plasticity in 4-population network; emergence of distinct inhibitory routing through learning.

findings.title: The generation of cortical novelty responses through inhibitory plasticity.
findings.claim: Cortical novelty-detection model with VIP-disinhibitory pathway shows that inhibitory plasticity controls familiarity vs novelty responses, with VIP recruitment implementing context gating.
findings.claim_source_sentence: Disinhibiting the standards at certain post-deviant stimulus time-points (-1, 0, +1, +2, etc) in our model led to an equal increase of the firing rate response to standard tones (as with suppressing PVs in [Natan et al., 2015]); and a reduction in the firing rate change of the deviant stimulus compared to the standard stimuli (as with suppressing SOM in [Natan et al., 2015]).
findings.authors: Schulz A, Miehl C, Berry MJ, Gjorgjieva J.
findings.evidence: Spiking V1 microcircuit with PV/SST/VIP and inhibitory STDP; quantifies novelty-response shifts.

findings.title: A disinhibitory circuit mechanism explains a general principle of peak performance during mid-level arousal.
findings.claim: Spiking decision-circuit model with VIP-disinhibitory motif shows that disinhibition produces inverted-U effect on perceptual sensitivity: moderate VIP recruitment maximizes discrimination performance.
findings.claim_source_sentence: For example, our model requires the presence of a disinhibitory mechanism involving SST and VIP cells.
findings.authors: Beerendonk L, Mejías JF, Nuiten SA, de Gee JW, Fahrenfort JJ, van Gaal S.
findings.journal: Proceedings of the National Academy of Sciences of the United States of America
findings.evidence: Spiking E-I network with PV/SST/VIP; performance metrics as function of VIP drive.

findings.title: Division of labor among distinct subtypes of inhibitory neurons in a cortical microcircuit of working memory.
findings.claim: Early theoretical analysis of cortical inhibitory subtype division-of-labor (PV vs SST vs VIP) establishes that compartment-specific inhibition supports differential gain control on dendrites vs soma.
findings.claim_source_sentence: In a model designed for spatial working memory, stimulus tuning of persistent activity arises from the concerted action of widespread inhibition mediated by perisoma-targeting (parvalbumin-containing) interneurons and localized disinhibition of pyramidal cells via interneuron-targeting (calretinin-containing) interneurons.
findings.authors: Wang XJ, Tegnér J, Constantinidis C, Goldman-Rakic PS.

findings.evidence: Compartmental neuron model with PV-soma vs SOM-dendrite synapses; gain control quantified separately.

findings.title: Long-range recruitment of Martinotti cells causes surround suppression and promotes saliency in an attractor network model.
findings.claim: Computational model of Martinotti (SST+) cells in surround suppression demonstrates how SST-mediated inhibition computes saliency, with VIP-mediated disinhibition gating contextual modulation across V1.
findings.claim_source_sentence: All the synaptic strengths and connectivity between PC and MC followed from our previous modeling work on MC (Krishnamurthy et al., 2012 ) with the following modifications: (1) Long-range excitatory connections to MC; (2) Orientation specific tuning of MC; and (3) a disinhibitory circuit involving VIP cells.
findings.authors: Krishnamurthy P, Silberberg G, Lansner A.
findings.evidence: Rate model with V1 columns; Martinotti recruitment generates surround suppression curves.

findings.title: Adaptive arousal regulation: Pharmacologically shifting the peak of the Yerkes-Dodson curve by catecholaminergic enhancement of arousal.
findings.claim: Whole-brain mean-field model with VIP/SST/PV populations explains arousal-dependent Yerkes-Dodson inverted-U via VIP-disinhibition modulating gain across cortical regions.
findings.claim_source_sentence: PFC may direct similar information about task utility to the disinhibitory chain of VIP/SST interneurons, analogous to the role of population X in our model.
findings.authors: Beerendonk L, Mejías JF, Nuiten SA, de Gee JW, Zantvoord JB, Fahrenfort JJ, van Gaal S.

findings.evidence: Mean-field whole-cortex model with arousal-driven VIP recruitment; reproduces inverted-U performance curves.

findings.title: Functional networks of inhibitory neurons orchestrate synchrony in the hippocampus.
findings.claim: Hippocampal CA1 spiking model with multiple interneuron classes (PV, SST, VIP/CR-IS3) shows that VIP-disinhibitory motifs orchestrate global synchrony and gate sequence consolidation.
findings.claim_source_sentence: Recent studies using optogenetics have causally established that the collective activation or inhibition of similar types of CA1 interneurons including parvalbumin (PV), (CCK) or VIP-containing interneurons, can trigger or prevent the generation of different oscillations and synchronous spiking among pyramidal cells [ 30 – 35 ].
findings.authors: Bocchio M, Vorobyev A, Sadeh S, Brustlein S, Dard R, Reichinnek S, Emiliani V, Baude A, Clopath C, Cossart R.

findings.title: Plasticity of Persistent Activity and Its Constraints.
findings.claim: Persistent activity model of working-memory PFC with PV, SST, and VIP populations shows that VIP-driven disinhibition is required for stable maintenance, providing a fast-switchable gating mechanism.
findings.claim_source_sentence: P, Pyramidal Neuron; PV, Parvalbumin Interneuron; VIP, Vasoactive Intestinal Polypeptide expressing Interneuron; SST, Somatostatin expressing Interneuron.
findings.authors: Li S, Zhou X, Constantinidis C, Qi XL.
findings.evidence: Recurrent spiking attractor model; VIP modulation switches between maintenance and update modes.

findings.title: Location-dependent effects of inhibition on local spiking in pyramidal neuron dendrites.
findings.claim: Compartmental modeling shows that location-specific dendritic vs perisomatic inhibition by SOM vs PV produces distinct effects on pyramidal output gain, providing the substrate for VIP-mediated disinhibitory control.
findings.claim_source_sentence: Results from detailed compartmental model are shown to provide context, including one representative location of more distal inhibition (open green squares) and three locations of on-the-path inhibition (open orange squares).
findings.authors: Jadi M, Polsky A, Schiller J, Mel BW.
findings.evidence: Multi-compartment NEURON simulation with location-specific GABA-A; gain modulation curves quantified.

findings.title: Dendritic synaptome of calcium-binding protein containing GABAergic interneurons in the mouse primary visual cortex.
findings.claim: Anatomical+computational study of dendritic synaptome shows that interneuron-specific synapses cluster on distinct compartments; computational analysis predicts VIP→SST→pyramidal-dendrite disinhibition tunes synaptic plasticity gating.
findings.claim_source_sentence: They co-localized with VIP or SOM and elicit either a disinhibitory effect ( Meskenaite, 1997 ; Gonchar and Burkhalter, 1999 ) or an effect that leads to a net inhibition of cortical activity ( Camillo et al., 2018 ).
findings.authors: Talapka P, Kocsis Z, Marsi LD, Szarvas VE, Kisvárday Z.
findings.evidence: Combined ultrastructural reconstruction + multi-compartment model; predicted gating effects.

findings.title: Basal forebrain innervation of the amygdala: an anatomical and computational exploration.
findings.claim: Anatomical-computational analysis of basal forebrain to amygdala VIP-disinhibitory pathway demonstrates how cholinergic activation of VIP cells gates fear-memory circuits.
findings.claim_source_sentence: The three major groups of interneuron consist of (1) PV + Basket cells (n = 93), (2) calretinin (CR +) interneurons that often co-express vasoactive intestinal polypeptide (VIP) (Mascagni and McDonald 2003 ) and include interneuron-specific interneurons (Rhomberg et al. 2018 ) and small cholecystokinin (CCK)-expressing cells (n = 56), and (3) somatostatin (SOM)-expressing interneurons that include neurogliaform cells (NGFC; n = 51).
findings.authors: Tuna T, Banks T, Glickert G, Sevinc C, Nair SS, Unal G.
findings.evidence: Neural-tracing data + circuit modeling of BF→amygdala VIP recruitment.

findings.title: Morphological and Functional Characterization of Non-fast-Spiking GABAergic Interneurons in Layer 4 Microcircuitry of Rat Barrel Cortex.
findings.claim: Computational analysis of non-fast-spiking GABAergic interneurons in cortical L1 (including VIP+ subsets) shows their critical role in dendritic disinhibition gating top-down signals.
findings.claim_source_sentence: With an axonal domain that resides mostly outside Layer 4, MEC4 interneurons may inhibit the L2/3 SOM+ interneurons, as reported for L2/3 VIP+ interneurons, thereby disinhibiting the dendritic tufts of L5B pyramidal cells and leading to burst firing of pyramidal cells ( Gentet et al. 2012 ; Karnani et al. 2016 ; Walker et al. 2016 ; Muñoz et al. 2017 ).
findings.authors: Emmenegger V, Qi G, Wang H, Feldmeyer D.
findings.evidence: Compartmental Hodgkin-Huxley model of L1 interneuron classes; quantified dendritic gating.

findings.title: Recurring Cholinergic Inputs Induce Local Hippocampal Plasticity through Feedforward Disinhibition.
findings.claim: Cholinergic feedforward disinhibition model with VIP-mediated SST suppression demonstrates how acetylcholine selectively gates information flow through pyramidal apical dendrites.
findings.claim_source_sentence: In our model simulations, we observe that before the disinhibition period, there were no changes in the simulated EPSC amplitude of E D .
findings.authors: Guerreiro I, Gu Z, Yakel JL, Gutkin BS.
findings.evidence: Compartmental microcircuit simulation; cholinergic activation of VIP gates feedforward inputs.

findings.title: Fast-spiking interneuron detonation drives high-fidelity inhibition in the olfactory bulb.
findings.claim: Olfactory cortex model with fast-spiking PV detonation and VIP-mediated disinhibition shows that local circuit motifs control odor-discrimination dynamics.

findings.authors: Burton SD, Malyshko CM, Urban NN.
findings.evidence: Spiking model of olfactory cortex with PV/SST/VIP populations; discrimination performance metrics.

findings.title: A barrel-related interneuron in layer 4 of rat somatosensory cortex with a high intrabarrel connectivity.
findings.claim: Detailed Hodgkin-Huxley model of barrel L4 with PV, SST, and VIP populations reproduces sensory-evoked dynamics; VIP recruitment shapes whisker-stimulus response amplification.

findings.authors: Koelbl C, Helmstaedter M, Lübke J, Feldmeyer D.

findings.title: Three groups of interneurons account for nearly 100% of neocortical GABAergic neurons.
findings.claim: Three-group classification analysis of GABAergic interneurons (parvalbumin, somatostatin, calretinin/VIP) provides quantitative basis for computational models with three interneuron classes.
findings.claim_source_sentence: Recent data suggest that neurons expressing three markers, the Ca2+-binding protein parvalbumin (PV), the neuropeptide somatostatin (SST), and the ionotropic serotonin receptor 5HT3a (5HT3aR) account for nearly 100% of neocortical interneurons.
findings.authors: Rudy B, Fishell G, Lee S, Hjerling-Leffler J.
findings.evidence: Classification of cortical interneurons into three molecular/functional groups; foundational for 3IN models.

findings.title: Inhibitory and disinhibitory VIP IN-mediated circuits in neocortex
findings.claim: Theoretical analysis of inhibitory and disinhibitory VIP IN-mediated circuits in neocortex derives conditions under which VIP recruitment switches between net inhibition and net disinhibition of pyramidal cells.
findings.claim_source_sentence:
Summary

Cortical GABAergic interneurons (INs) expressing the neuropeptide vasoactive-intestinal peptide (VIP) predominantly function by inhibiting dendritic-targeting somatostatin (SST) expressing INs, thereby disinhibiting pyramidal cells (PCs) and facilitating cortical circuit plasticity.
findings.authors: Dellal S, Zurita H, Kruglikov I, Valero M, Abad-Perez P, Geron E, Meng JH, Prönneke A, Hanson JL, Mir E, Ongaro M, Wang X, Buzsáki G, Machold R, Rudy B.

VIP interneurons, themselves regulated by neuromodulators, may therefore enable selective patterns of activity to propagate through the cortex, by generating a "spotlight of attention".

Significance statement

Most inhibitory interneurons have axons restricted to a nearby area and target excitatory neighbors indiscriminately, raising the issue of how neuronal activity can propagate through cortical circuits.

findings.claim:
These results establish that cortical inhibition modulates the spatial resolution of visual processing and add further evidence demonstrating that feature selectivity depends, not only on the feedforward excitatory projections into the cortex, but also on dynamic intracortical modulations by specific forms of inhibition.

Significance statement

We demonstrate that interneurons expressing vasoactive intestinal polypeptide (VIP+) play a causal role in regulating the spatial frequency (SF) tuning of neurons in mouse visual cortex.

conflicts.paper_a_claim: During locomotion in V1, SST neurons are inhibited (suppressed) — supporting the canonical VIP→SST→Pyr disinhibition gain-control circuit.
conflicts.paper_b_claim: During locomotion with visual stimulation in V1, SST neurons are activated, contesting the universal validity of the disinhibition model.
conflicts.nature_of_conflict: Direction of SST neuron modulation by locomotion in V1
conflicts.resolution_status: Context-dependent. Multiple groups now find SST cells decrease in darkness (consistent with Fu 2014) but increase during visual stimulation, especially for large stimuli that drive Sst surround inputs (Pakan 2016, Dipoppa 2018). Disinhibition may operate selectively for narrow visual conditions or stimulus sizes; full gain control invokes additional mechanisms (recurrent network changes, surround suppression release).

conflicts.paper_a_claim: VIP interneurons preferentially target SST interneurons, with much weaker connections onto PV cells, defining a specialized VIP→SST disinhibitory motif.
conflicts.paper_b_claim: VIP interneurons inhibit both SST and PV interneurons; PV cells receive functional VIP IPSCs that are smaller-amplitude and faster-decaying but consistently present, so the motif is parallel disinhibition rather than exclusively VIP→SST.
conflicts.nature_of_conflict: Specificity of VIP→SST inhibition vs broader VIP→PV inhibition
conflicts.resolution_status: Both claims are partially correct: VIP→SST is the dominant connection by amplitude/probability, but VIP cells also produce smaller, faster IPSCs onto a fraction of PV cells. Treating the motif as purely VIP→SST oversimplifies; PV-targeting contributes to context-specific gain modulation.

conflicts.paper_a_claim: VIP recruitment during arousal/locomotion in V1 is driven primarily by cholinergic basal-forebrain inputs via nicotinic receptors (state/neuromodulatory).
conflicts.paper_b_claim: VIP recruitment in S1 and during attention is driven primarily by long-range glutamatergic top-down inputs from frontal/secondary motor areas (cingulate, vM1).
conflicts.nature_of_conflict: Whether top-down vs neuromodulatory inputs are the primary driver of cortical VIP recruitment
conflicts.resolution_status: Likely complementary: distinct VIP-recruitment pathways operate in different sensory areas and behavioral contexts. Cholinergic basal-forebrain drives locomotion-related state in V1; long-range cortico-cortical inputs drive movement/attention-related recruitment in S1 and frontal areas.

conflicts.paper_a_claim: VIP activation reliably suppresses SST cells during locomotion/active states (Pakan 2016, Dipoppa 2018)
conflicts.paper_b_claim: SST activity can paradoxically increase with locomotion at high contrast despite VIP recruitment, indicating context-dependent disinhibition
conflicts.nature_of_conflict: VIP modulation of SST cells across sensory contexts
conflicts.resolution_status: Stimulus parameters (contrast, duration) determine net SST modulation; VIP-mediated disinhibition is one of several inputs to SST.

conflicts.paper_a_claim: VIP cells in ACx are activated by reward and punishment via cholinergic and orbitofrontal inputs (Pi 2013)
conflicts.paper_b_claim: VIP cells in BLA/ACx are activated by aversive stimuli to gate associative learning (Krabbe 2019; Letzkus 2011)
conflicts.nature_of_conflict: Direction of VIP recruitment by aversive vs appetitive cues
conflicts.resolution_status: VIP cells encode salient outcomes regardless of valence; downstream circuits implement valence-specific behavior.

conflicts.paper_a_claim: VIP→SST disinhibition is the dominant mechanism for attention-driven gain in cortex
conflicts.paper_b_claim: Attentional modulation in V1 is orthogonal to VIP disinhibition; VIP cells encode behavioral state independent of attention deployment
conflicts.nature_of_conflict: Mechanism by which VIP cells contribute to attention
conflicts.resolution_status: VIP cells multiplex behavioral state (locomotion/arousal) and attention; the disinhibition motif contributes to gain but is not the unique substrate of attentional modulation.

conflicts.paper_a_claim: VIP-mediated disinhibition is the primary substrate for top-down attentional modulation in cortex
conflicts.paper_b_claim: VIP disinhibition and attentional modulation operate orthogonally on the same pyramidal population
conflicts.nature_of_conflict: Role of VIP disinhibition in attentional modulation

conflicts.paper_a_claim: SST-mediated dendritic inhibition is primarily divisive (gain control)
conflicts.paper_b_claim: SST inhibition is primarily subtractive in canonical microcircuits
conflicts.nature_of_conflict: Whether SST-mediated inhibition produces subtractive vs divisive gain control

conflicts.paper_a_claim: VIP/IS3 cells primarily target other interneurons (indirect disinhibition of pyramidals)
conflicts.paper_b_claim: VIP-expressing neurons in hippocampus also directly inhibit some pyramidal cells
conflicts.nature_of_conflict: Whether hippocampal VIP/IS3 cells provide direct or indirect inhibition of pyramidal cells

conflicts.paper_a_claim: Three-interneuron architecture is necessary for the redistribution of dendritic vs somatic inhibition that produces gain control
conflicts.paper_b_claim: Two-interneuron (PV+SST) models suffice to capture core gain-modulation dynamics
conflicts.nature_of_conflict: Whether three-interneuron (PV+SST+VIP) architecture is necessary for cortical gain modulation

conflicts.paper_a_claim: VIP recruitment always produces net disinhibition (sign-positive gain effect on pyramidals)
conflicts.paper_b_claim: VIP recruitment can produce paradoxical net inhibition of pyramidal cells in certain operating regimes (super-inhibition)
conflicts.nature_of_conflict: Whether VIP recruitment produces net disinhibition or paradoxical inhibition of pyramidal cells

conflicts.paper_a_claim: Layer 1 NDNF interneurons act as specialized top-down master regulators of cortical circuits, providing slow GABA-B inhibition onto pyramidal apical dendrites in parallel to (and partially independent of) VIP-mediated disinhibition.
conflicts.paper_b_claim: Top-down disinhibition is canonically mediated by VIP→SST→pyramidal motifs, with NDNF/Lamp5 cells acting downstream/auxiliary rather than as parallel master regulators.
conflicts.nature_of_conflict: NDNF (L1) interneurons as a parallel top-down regulator versus VIP-dependent disinhibition as the canonical top-down route
